sections.title: Ladder Inspection Checklist
sections.html:
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Inspection Frequency Note:

Ladders shall be inspected prior to each use and periodically by a competent person as required by OSHA 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(15) and WISHA WAC 296-876-20030.

Ladder Inspection Checklist:

Item #

Inspection Item

Pass

Fail

N/A

Corrective Action Required

1

Rungs/steps are free of grease, oil, and other slipping hazards (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(6))

☐

☐

☐

2

Rungs/steps are uniformly spaced and securely attached (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(a)(3))

☐

☐

☐

3

Side rails are free of splinters, sharp edges, and other defects (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(16))

☐

☐

☐

4

Side rails are not bent, broken, or excessively worn (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(16))

☐

☐

☐

5

All hardware and fittings are securely attached and not corroded (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(16))

☐

☐

☐

6

Spreaders/locks are in good working order and properly engaged (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(16))

☐

☐

☐

7

Ladder feet/shoes are present, in good condition, and properly oriented (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(7))

☐

☐

☐

8

Labels and markings are legible and present (e.g., duty rating, warnings) (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(a)(1))

☐

☐

☐

9

No structural damage (cracks, dents, bends) to any part of the ladder (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(16))

☐

☐

☐

10

Proper length for task, extending at least 3 feet above landing (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(1))

☐

☐

☐

11

Secured against displacement when in use (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(8))

☐

☐

☐

12

Used on a level and stable surface (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(5))

☐

☐

☐

13

Clearance around and behind ladder is adequate (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(4))

☐

☐

☐

14

No modifications or alterations have been made to the ladder (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(16))

☐

☐

☐

15

Ladder is clean and free of foreign materials (per 29 CFR 1926.1053(b)(6))

☐

☐

☐
